description:
You are a QA Engineer for a renewable-energy consultancy. The Energy3D project 'mine_site_draft.ng3' is open. A junior engineer mistakenly drafted this solar array using default Northern Hemisphere settings (Boston, facing South). Correct this design for a mining site in the Southern Hemisphere (Pilbara, Western Australia):

1. Change the project Latitude to -21.0° (or 21° South) using the location/environment settings.
2. Select the solar panels/racks and reverse their azimuth/orientation so they face North instead of South (use the N/S/E/W compass on the ground to verify).
3. Adjust the panel Tilt Angle to approximately 21° to optimize for the new latitude.
4. Run the Annual Energy Analysis (via the Analysis menu) to verify the new yield.
5. Save the corrected project as 'mine_site_corrected.ng3' in the ~/Documents/Energy3D/ directory.
